frame_analyses.description: Initial setup showing Penrose drain on gauze with marked targets visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard EMIG L4 setup with Penrose drain positioned at 45-degree angle

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver approaching suture needle for loading
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper approach with needle driver from appropriate angle

frame_analyses.description: Needle being passed through Penrose drain at first marked target
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle control and appropriate entry angle through drain material

frame_analyses.description: Completing first bite through drain, suture being pulled through
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth passage with adequate suture length maintained

frame_analyses.description: Positioning for second bite at opposite marked target
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appropriate spacing and positioning for second pass

frame_analyses.description: Beginning first throw with intracorporeal technique
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand positioning for surgeon's knot initiation

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw with appropriate tension
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good loop formation and tightening technique

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being executed with opposite hand
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand alternation and single throw technique

frame_analyses.description: Final knot secured, suture ends visible but not yet cut
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears well-secured with good tissue approximation

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw
Good single throw with hand alternation
Final single throw completing secure knot

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Excellent closure of Penrose drain slit with proper tissue approximation and no visible gaps

technique_summary: This appears to be a demonstration video showing exemplary EMIG L4 technique. The suture placement was precise at marked targets, knot construction followed proper sequence (surgeon's knot plus two single throws with hand alternation), and final closure was complete without gaps.
improvement_suggestions: This is demonstration-quality technique with no significant areas for improvement visible
strengths: Perfect suture placement at marked targets
